narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD weakness because DXY down 0.42% with USD scoring zero on CSM, driving broad anti-dollar flows across majors
2. Risk-on equity and commodity surge because S&P 500 up 0.84% and copper up 2.76% fuel high-beta currency outperformance led by AUD and NZD
3. H4 momentum divergence from D1 because H4 regime is risk-on high with leading signals in GBPUSD, AUDUSD, and AUDJPY while daily remains in mixed transition, meaning the shorter frame is actively setting direction

PRICE CONFIRMS

AUDUSD and GBPUSD are leading the anti-dollar move with H4 buy signals at plus four and strong CSM scores of 84 and implied GBP strength respectively, consistent with their tight 0.88 and 0.93 correlations to EURUSD which is also tracking lower in USD terms. USDCHF confirming with an H4 sell signal at minus four and CHF topping CSM at 100 validates the dollar selling story structurally. CADJPY is lagging as expected given CAD scores only 14 on CSM, and its negative signal at minus four reflects JPY outperforming within the yen-cross complex despite USDJPY softness.

REGIME

Risk-on confirmed but with one complication. VIX at 17.19 is elevated and ticked up 0.64%, S&P 500 is up 0.84% supporting risk appetite, and Gold up 0.66% introduces mild safe-haven ambiguity, but copper surge at plus 2.76% alongside Bitcoin and equities outweighs the gold signal as the dominant read.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Gold versus risk-on: Gold rising alongside equities and copper suggests residual safe-haven demand that conflicts with a clean risk-on label and keeps conviction capped
2. VIX rising while equities rally: VIX up 0.64% on a strong equity session implies hedging activity beneath the surface, conflicting with the purely bullish macro overlay score

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: AUDUSD up - CHF and AUD lead CSM, USD zero, H4 buy at plus four confirmed - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: AUDJPY up - risk-on equity surge supports yen-funded high-beta longs, H4 buy confirmed - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: USDCAD - weakest correlation structure at minus 0.56 and noisy CAD CSM at 14 create conflicted signals
Watch: A VIX break above 18.50 invalidates the risk-on regime and collapses the AUDUSD and AUDJPY long thesis immediately
key_observation: Strong risk-on sentiment with equity gains and commodity rebounds. Iran peace proposal introduces geopolitical tail risk reduction. Agricultural strength suggests inflation concerns persist. Precious metals show divergence between gold and silver, indicating selective demand.
watch: USD weakness if risk-on continues; potential safe-haven demand reversal if geopolitical tensions ease further; agricultural commodity trajectory for inflation implications.
